states_list.name: INIT
states_list.description: Initial connection state before any command

states_list.name: DATA_CHANNEL_READY
states_list.description: Data connection parameters negotiated; waiting for a transfer command

states_list.name: RENAME_PENDING
states_list.description: Rename source accepted, waiting for RNTO

invariants_list.rule: The PASS command must be sent immediately following a USER command.
Commands like LIST, RETR, and STOR require a successful authentication (230 logged in) state before execution.
A data connection setup command (PASV, PORT, EPSV, or EPRT) should be sent before initiating a data transfer (LIST, RETR, STOR, etc.).
If a 530 response is received after PASS, the client should restart the authentication sequence with USER.
The RNTO command must be preceded by an RNFR command.
The QUIT command is valid at any point in the session to terminate the connection.
CWD.path: Path to file or directory.
RETR.filename: Name of the file to retrieve.
STOR.filename: Name of the file to store.

recommended_actions: Next iteration should emphasize broader data-channel and post-auth file-operation seeds to improve message-to-transition coverage.

suggested_campaign.objective: Increase post-auth and data-channel coverage while validating weaker transitions.

fuzzing_observation.explanation: Next iteration should emphasize broader data-channel and post-auth file-operation seeds to improve message-to-transition coverage.
